description: Automatic invoices renders one parent group for a shared `client + invoice window` instead of separate top-level rows for each child candidate.
Parent rows display child count, aggregate amount, and invoice window summary correctly.
Expanding a parent row reveals child candidates with contract or assignment identity, cadence source, service period, and amount.
A parent row is combinable only when all ready children share the same client, currency, PO scope, tax source, and export shape.
A parent row becomes non-combinable when PO scope differs across children and shows the PO incompatibility reason.
A parent row becomes non-combinable when currency differs across children and shows the currency incompatibility reason.
A parent row becomes non-combinable when tax source differs across children and shows the tax incompatibility reason.
A parent row becomes non-combinable when export shape differs across children and shows the export incompatibility reason.
When a parent is combinable, selecting the parent selects the whole group as one invoice target.
When a parent is non-combinable, the parent checkbox is disabled while child rows remain individually selectable.
Partial child selection drives a tri-state parent indicator without silently changing execution semantics.
`Select All` selects combinable groups by parent row instead of selecting each child individually.
`Select All` selects child rows individually for non-combinable groups instead of attempting invalid parent selection.
Blocked child rows remain visible inside a group and do not become selectable through either child selection or parent `Select All`.
Previewing a selected combinable parent renders one combined invoice preview and states `This selection will generate 1 invoice`.
Previewing a non-combinable mixed child selection renders a multi-invoice preview state or summary with the correct invoice count.
Preview executes against the exact selected parent or child scope and does not include unselected sibling candidates from the same group.
Generating a selected combinable parent creates exactly one invoice spanning all selected compatible children.
Generating selected child rows from a non-combinable group creates multiple invoices while preserving the exact selected scope.
Grouped generation does not re-expand into unselected siblings when the same client and invoice window contain additional candidates.
Duplicate-prevention logic blocks re-invoicing the same grouped parent selection or member selection without blocking unrelated siblings.
A combined multi-assignment invoice persists successfully without requiring a fake invoice-header `client_contract_id` owner.
Charge persistence preserves `client_contract_id` attribution per charge for a combined multi-assignment invoice.
Invoice reads and invoice-detail rendering expose enough per-charge assignment attribution to explain a combined multi-assignment invoice.
Invoice history shows a combined grouped invoice clearly and does not mislabel it as belonging to only one assignment when it spans several.
Purchase-order enforcement keeps a parent non-combinable when child candidates do not share compatible PO scope.
A valid no-PO grouped combination can generate as one invoice without tripping header-assignment PO checks.
Legacy single-assignment parent groups still preview and generate exactly as before.
A single-child group still behaves like the legacy one-row flow and remains fully supported by preview and generation.
DB-backed integration proves two active contracts for the same client with no PO constraint can be selected as one grouped parent and generated as one invoice.
DB-backed integration proves two active contracts for the same client with incompatible PO scope remain grouped visually but require separate child-level invoicing.
DB-backed integration proves `Select All` combines compatible groups and fans out incompatible groups in the same run without cross-contamination.
DB-backed integration proves grouped duplicate protection remains correct when a prior combined invoice exists for part of the same client window.
Static/wiring coverage proves in-product copy, docs, and runbook text describe parent grouping, disabled non-combinable parents, and smart `Select All` semantics.
